QF.id: XC Investment Products and Market Developments_1

question.text: Under SEBI regulations, a Portfolio Management Service (PMS) requires a minimum investment of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates a minimum investment of Rs.50 lakh for PMS clients. This threshold was revised upward from Rs.25 lakh to Rs.50 lakh to ensure only suitable investors access this product. **The Trap:** Rs.25 lakh was the old threshold. Rs.1 crore is the AIF minimum. Rs.10 lakh is neither. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A Chapter 12 covers PMS. Series XXI-A and XXI-B are dedicated PMS exams with detailed threshold testing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients who don't meet the Rs.50 lakh PMS threshold should be guided toward mutual funds which offer professional management with no minimum investment barrier.
